subtasks.description: Query TPC-H schema to identify top 10 customers by lifetime value (total revenue) in market segment 'BUILDING'. Return customer ID, name, segment, and total lifetime value, ordered by LTV descending.
For each of the top 10 customers, retrieve their order pattern: order dates, frequencies, order values, product categories, and temporal distribution across their account lifecycle.

Reconcile customer LTV data with order pattern data, synthesize a personalized retention narrative for each customer that references their specific ordering behavior, frequency trends, seasonal patterns, and value contributions. Output 10 narratives with customer context.

plan.cost_rationale: Plan uses 3 subtasks (minimum required). Two independent SQL queries (sql_ltv and sql_order_pattern) are separated because they have different WHERE/GROUP BY logic and result schemas, but both are mid-tier complexity (multi-table joins with aggregation). The cross_recon node is frontier-tier because it must synthesize narrative prose from two data streams, infer customer behavior patterns, and generate personalized retention messaging—a task requiring high contextual reasoning and language generation. Total cost is justified by the analytical complexity and personalization requirement.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, c.c_mktsegment, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS lifetime_value FROM customer c JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name, c.c_mktsegment ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10

WITH top_customers AS (SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS lifetime_value FROM customer c JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10) SELECT tc.c_custkey, tc.c_name, tc.lifetime_value, o.o_orderkey, o.o_orderdate, o.o_totalprice, o.o_orderstatus, o.o_orderpriority, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.c_custkey) AS total_orders, MIN(o.o_orderdate) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.c_custkey) AS first_order_date, MAX(o.o_orderdate) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.c_custkey) AS last_order_date, AVG(o.o_totalprice) OVER (PARTITION BY tc.c_custkey) AS avg_order_value FROM top_customers tc JOIN orders o ON tc.c_custkey = o.o_custkey ORDER BY tc.lifetime_value DESC, o.o_orderdate
